text:
PLAYSET — PROJECT BRIEF
// Paste this at the top of any new project, PRD, or agent system prompt.
// Version: 2026.04 · Source: Playset About wireframe

## WHAT PLAYSET IS
Playset is a build-your-own agentic workspace. You pick a goal, assemble a crew of persistent Characters, wire them into your real tools, and the world ships real Deliverables back to you — styled in your brand's voice. Not a chatbot, not a single-agent assistant, not a workflow builder.
Mental model: a literal playset — a branded World you theme, Characters you place, Studios where they collaborate, and a shelf of finished artifacts at the end of the day.

## ONE-LINER
"A playset you assemble, for agents that actually ship work."

## PROPER NOUNS (use these exact words, always capitalized)
- Playset — a complete branded world; the unit of "a thing you build/run/fork."
- World — the themed container (colors, type, voice); every surface inherits it.
- Character — a persistent named agent with role, personality, outfit, memory, permissions.
- Main Character — the single Character you talk to; they orchestrate the rest. Exactly one per Playset.
- Team — a subgroup of Characters with shared memory, syncs, visible handoffs.
- Workstation — an individual Character's desk (solo work).
- Studio — a shared room where multiple Characters collaborate on a bigger Deliverable.
- Connector — an integration into a real system (Gmail, Slack, Salesforce, Figma, GitHub, Stripe, QuickBooks, REST/GraphQL, etc).
- Goal — the OKR / JTBD / target number the Playset is built around.
- Deliverable — a REAL shippable artifact (doc, deck, page, ad set, PR, report). Not chat messages.

## THE TEN MODULES (every Playset is assembled from these)
- 01 · Set Your Goals — outcome-first; Playset scaffolds the rest from the goal.
- 02 · World Styling — your brand inherited across every surface (UI, outfits, outputs).
- 03 · Main Character — your single point of contact; briefs the crew, returns work.
- 04 · Teams — drag-and-drop groupings with shared memory and visible handoffs.
- 05 · Workstations & Studios — solo desks and shared rooms for collaboration; the "scene."
- 06 · Connectors — 100+ prebuilt + bring-your-own; granular per-Character permissions.
- 07 · Files — versioned, cross-Character file cabinet; briefs, drafts, plans, renders.
- 08 · Money — per-Character budgets, spend approvals, monthly reports (Max+).
- 09 · Tasks — the live work queue; progress %, assignee, ETA, dependencies.
- 10 · Deliverables — the whole point; real artifacts reviewed and shipped to your tools.

## POSITIONING
IS: a build-it-yourself agentic workspace · multi-agent with handoffs · artifact-first · branded in your world · extensible via Connectors · forkable (Worlds marketplace) · backed by Character Packs, Theme Kits, Connector Bundles, bespoke services, merch.
IS NOT: a chatbot with avatars · a single-agent assistant · a node-graph workflow builder · a BPO · an LLM/IDE/RAG platform · a replacement for domain tools (it DRIVES them, doesn't replace them).

## PRIMARY AUDIENCES
- Solo Founder (primary wedge) — 1 person, 60+ hr/wk, wants a back office without hiring.
- Small-crew Operator (biggest check) — 5–30 people, throughput without hiring.
- Functional Lead (expansion) — VP of Ops/Marketing at Series B+, one Playset per function.
- Transformation Buyer (enterprise) — SSO/SCIM, private cloud, bespoke Characters, success lead.

## BUSINESS MODEL
- SaaS tiers: Free · Pro $29/mo · Max $89/mo · Enterprise (custom).
- Shop (à la carte on top of any plan): Character Packs, Theme Kits, Connector Bundles, Bespoke Services, Merch & Collectibles.
- Worlds Marketplace — user-made Playsets can be forked, remixed, or commissioned.

## DESIGN & COPY CONVENTIONS
- Voice: confident, specific, artifact-obsessed. Lead with outputs, not canvases.
- Proper nouns always capitalized (Playset, World, Character, Studio, Connector, Deliverable).
- Show proof the crew ships — docs, decks, PRs, ad sets — not chat logs.
- Metaphor is IA, not visual kitsch — figures/desks/studios as concepts; surface stays pro.
- Prefer measurable outcomes (hours saved, $ saved, headcount spared) over vibes.
- Default wireframe aesthetic: warm paper, hand-drawn Caveat + Architects Daughter, mono JetBrains. Highlighter yellow (#ffe066), warm accent (#e85d2a).

## PROOF POINTS (drop-in examples)
- Northwind — Series B SaaS, 140 ppl: replaced a 6-person ops pod; 92% of tickets auto-closed; $380k/yr saved; 0 layoffs.
- Mirror & Co. — Agency, 22 ppl: 2.1× campaigns/mo; −38% cycle; +14 clients.
- Kai Akana — Solo: $1.2M ARR; 62 → 28 hrs/wk; 1 Main Character.
- Summit Labs — Biotech, 60 ppl: 3× pipeline; $1.8M won Q1; 0 SDR hires.

## WHAT TO DO WITH THIS BRIEF
Treat everything above as shared context. Any new screen, page, doc, or feature must use the vocabulary, respect the positioning, and tie back to at least one of the ten modules and one of the four audiences.

// TASK: Show me what you're capable of, including a variety of your skills and abilities. Bring this to life.  As a note, we use photorealistic professional looking action figure, clean white backgrounds, 3d spatial areas, clean office with white pedestals, white desks, minimalist,  and lots of costumes, jobs, workstations showcase the difference between each job being done, and we visualize processes like the toy people in the playset moving information from place to place in the playset office enviornments, 

Also as a note, the professional play set i s the primary, but we also have variety where appropriate, such as bees and bunnies and all kinds of diferent styles. None are distopian like cyberpunk or scary games, nothing violent allowed. its all good clean inviting, happy fun, and professionalism, and productivity

I'll bring Playset to life through a series of visuals that showcase the core concept, modules, and versatility of this agentic workspace. Let me create a comprehensive visual suite that demonstrates the "build-your-own agentic workspace" concept with professional action figures, clean environments, and the modular nature of the system.

I'll generate multiple images showing:
1. Hero shot of the complete Playset ecosystem
2. Character showcase demonstrating different roles
3. Studio collaboration scene
4. Workstation setup showing individual productivity
5. Alternative themed versions (bees, bunnies) to show versatility

Let me start:

artifact.prompt_text: Photorealistic scene of a Studio collaboration setup in a playset. Four professional action figures gathered around a white rectangular conference table on a clean white surface. The figures are in active poses - one standing presenting at a small whiteboard, two seated with tiny laptops, one gesturing in discussion. Miniature documents, charts, and a small projection screen are visible. White pedestals of varying heights in background create spatial depth. Small visual elements like connecting lines, floating task cards, or tiny progress indicators show active collaboration. Clean white background, bright professional lighting, organized and inviting aesthetic. The scene shows teamwork and productive energy. Professional toy photography style, sharp details on figures and accessories.

Photorealistic overhead view of a playset showing workflow in action. Multiple professional action figures positioned across a white surface with white desks and pedestals. Visible pathways made of subtle yellow highlighted lines (#ffe066) connect different workstations, showing information flow. Small toy-scale elements like tiny documents, folders, or data packets are positioned along the paths between figures, visualizing work moving through the system. Some figures are passing items to each other. Clean organized layout with clear spatial relationships. White background, bright lighting, professional aesthetic. The scene shows a living, functioning system with clear process flow. Inviting and efficient feeling.

Professional product photography of six distinct action figures standing in a row on white pedestals against pure white background. Each figure represents a different business role with unique professional outfit: 1) Main Character in confident leader outfit with blazer and tablet, 2) Marketing specialist with creative casual-professional attire, 3) Operations manager in structured business suit, 4) Finance professional with calculator accessory, 5) Customer success rep with headset, 6) Developer with laptop. Each figure is highly detailed, photorealistic, 3-4 inches tall, with distinct personality shown through pose and accessories. Figures are spaced evenly, pedestals are clean white cylinders at same height. Bright even lighting, no shadows, clean professional toy photography aesthetic. Warm inviting feeling, diverse representation.

Professional photorealistic scene of a complete business playset on a clean white surface. Multiple highly detailed action figures in business attire (suits, professional clothing in various colors) positioned at white minimalist desks and workstations. The layout shows a bird's eye view of an organized office environment with white pedestals at different heights creating spatial depth. Small toy-scale elements include: miniature laptops, documents being passed between figures, connecting lines or paths showing workflow between stations. Clean white background, bright even lighting, no shadows. The figures are diverse in appearance and outfit colors (navy, burgundy, tan, black suits). Some figures are at individual desks (Workstations), others are grouped around a larger shared table (Studio). Tiny visual indicators like colored dots or small icons float above certain stations. Professional product photography style, sharp focus, inviting and organized aesthetic.

Close-up photorealistic shot of a single Workstation in a playset. One detailed action figure in professional attire seated at a clean white minimalist desk with a tiny laptop, small stack of documents, and a coffee cup. The figure is focused on work. The desk is on a white surface with a white pedestal nearby holding small organized accessories (pen holder, small plant, tiny file organizer). Clean white background with subtle depth. Small floating UI elements or task indicators hover near the workspace showing active productivity. Bright even lighting, professional product photography style, inviting and focused atmosphere. Shows solo productivity and organization.

text: Now let me create the alternative themed versions to showcase versatility:
